triage_items.evidence: Full-suite run raises AssertionError('subprocess.run must never be called in this suite') from _SubprocessGuard defined in test_command_runner.py (introduced Stage 5.1, commit f7fc236). That module performs an unscoped module-level monkeypatch `command_runner.subprocess.run = _GUARD` with no fixture teardown, which leaks into later-collected test modules within the same pytest session. Running `pytest scos/control_center/tests/test_stage5_final_certification.py -q` in isolation yields 14 passed, 0 failed -- confirming the failure is caused by cross-file test-order pollution, not by any code path this test exercises.
triage_items.recommended_action: File a Stage 6.x test-hygiene follow-up to scope the subprocess monkeypatch in test_command_runner.py to a fixture with teardown (e.g. monkeypatch.setattr) instead of a bare module-level assignment. Not required before Stage 6.4.

triage_items.evidence: Same _SubprocessGuard cross-file pollution as test_certification_id (see above). Passes in isolation.
triage_items.recommended_action: Covered by the shared test_command_runner.py fixture-scoping follow-up.

triage_items.evidence: ERROR at setup: fixture 'tmp_dir' not found (available fixtures include tmp_path, tmp_path_factory, tmpdir but not tmp_dir). Test file last modified in Stage 5.4 (commit 93da717, 'add Stage 5.4 prompt result packets'), long before Stage 6.3's SQLite WAL work. The referenced local fixture was never defined/renamed in this file and the bug predates Stage 6.2/6.3/6.4 entirely.
triage_items.recommended_action: File a Stage 6.x test-hygiene follow-up to rename the `tmp_dir` fixture parameter to `tmp_path` (or add a local `tmp_dir` fixture alias) in test_prompt_result_packet_store.py. Not required before Stage 6.4.

triage_items.evidence: Same missing 'tmp_dir' fixture defect as test_append_and_load_prompt_packets_roundtrip (see above). Pre-existing since Stage 5.4.
triage_items.recommended_action: Covered by the shared tmp_dir/tmp_path fixture follow-up.
